entities.name: Francis Geisdorff
entities.context: Named in patent title notation as 'Francis Geisdorfe -patent-Illegal, 1919'

entities.name: Frederick Geisdorff Jr.
entities.context: Crow allottee #2336, deceased, allotted land in Crow Reservation Montana in 1907, died November 27, 1918 of influenza aged about 20

entities.name: Frederick Geisdorff Sr.
entities.context: Father of Frederick Geisdorff Jr., received leasing papers from Superintendent Asbury in August 1918

entities.name: Emily J. Geisdorff
entities.context: Widow of Frederick Geisdorff Jr., living at Randlett/Fort Duchesne Utah, heir to estate

entities.name: Emily M. Geisdorff
entities.context: Widow of Frederick Geisdorff Jr., also referenced as Emily M. Geisdorff in some correspondence

entities.name: Emily F. Geisdorff
entities.context: Widow of Frederick Geisdorff Jr., also referenced as Emily F. Geisdorff in some correspondence

entities.name: Emily Lucile Geisdorff
entities.context: Minor daughter of Frederick Geisdorff Jr. and Emily J. Geisdorff, born May 23, 1918, heir to estate

entities.name: Thomas R. Powers
entities.context: Purchaser of Frederick Geisdorff Jr.'s allotment, of Wyola Montana and Hartman Wyoming, bid $1500 for 159.67 acres

entities.name: C. H. Asbury
entities.context: Superintendent of Crow Agency, Montana, handled land sale correspondence

entities.name: Albert H. Kneale
entities.context: Superintendent of Uintah and Ouray Agency, Fort Duchesne Utah, assisted with sale matters and notified Crow Agency of Frederick's death

entities.name: Calvin Coolidge
entities.context: President of the United States who signed the trust patent for Francis Geisdorff on December 21, 1923

entities.name: Woodrow Wilson
entities.context: President of the United States who signed fee patent number 719146 on November 14, 1919 to Thomas R. Powers

entities.name: John McPhaul
entities.context: Acting Assistant Commissioner of General Land Office, signed statement October 13, 1919 that no reservations or withdrawals covered the land

entities.name: Eloise Whitebear Pease
entities.context: Named on collection stamps throughout the document, collector/owner of these historical documents

entities.name: Crow Agency
entities.context: U.S. Indian Service agency in Montana handling allotment and land sale matters

entities.name: Uintah and Ouray Agency
entities.context: U.S. Indian Service agency at Fort Duchesne Utah, jurisdiction over Frederick Geisdorff Jr. and his widow

entities.name: Department of the Interior
entities.context: Federal department overseeing Indian affairs and land patents

entities.name: Crow Tribe of Indians
entities.context: Native American tribe whose reservation land was subject to allotment; argued to be rightful owner upon allottee's death

entities.name: Merchants National Bank of Billings
entities.context: Bank in Billings Montana holding $7.98 lease money deposited to credit of Frederick Geisdorff Jr.'s estate

entities.name: Sheridan Banking Company
entities.context: Bank on which Thomas R. Powers' checks for $1350 and $150 were drawn, dated November 2, 1918

entities.name: Crow Reservation
entities.context: Montana Indian reservation where Frederick Geisdorff Jr.'s allotment was located

entities.name: Crow Agency, Montana
entities.context: Location of the Crow Agency administrative office

entities.context: Location of Uintah and Ouray Agency and residence of Frederick Geisdorff Jr. and his widow

entities.context: Residence of Frederick Geisdorff Jr. and his widow Emily

entities.context: Residence of Thomas R. Powers

entities.context: Address of Thomas R. Powers in December 1918 correspondence

entities.name: Washington, D.C.

entities.name: Allotment No. 2336
entities.context: Frederick Geisdorff Jr.'s Crow allotment, S1/2 NE, SE1/4 NW1/4 and Lot 2, Section 19, T.9S, R.34E, 159.67 acres

entities.name: Crow Land Sale No. 991
entities.context: Official land sale number for the sale of Frederick Geisdorff Jr.'s allotment to Thomas R. Powers

entities.name: Frederick Geisdorff Jr. allotment - Francis Geisdorff trust patent
entities.context: Lots 9,10,11,12 of Section 2 and N1/2 SW1/4, N1/2 SE1/4, N1/2 S1/2 SE1/4 of Section 11, T.1S, R.34E Montana Meridian, 648.04 acres

entities.context: Act under which Francis Geisdorff's homestead allotment was made

entities.name: Act of April 11, 1882 (22 Stat. L. 42)
entities.context: Act under which Frederick Geisdorff Jr.'s allotment was made

entities.name: Act of February 8, 1887 (24 Stat. L. 388)
entities.context: Dawes Act under which trust patent for Frederick Geisdorff Jr.'s allotment was issued December 2, 1907

entities.context: Act referenced in fee patent conditions for Thomas R. Powers

entities.context: Referenced as basis for Crow Tribe's claim to land ownership upon allottee's death

entities.name: Heirship Estate of Frederick Geisdorff Jr.
entities.context: Probate/heirship determination for deceased Crow allottee #2336, hearing held August 29, 1919

entities.name: Geisdorff family allotments
entities.context: Multiple allotments belonging to Frederick Geisdorff Jr.'s brother, sister, and three other sisters adjacent to his allotment, totaling several hundred acres of grazing land

events.description: Frederick Geisdorff Jr. allotted 159.67 acres (S1/2 NE, SE1/4 NW1/4, Lot 2, Section 19, T.9S, R.34E) as Crow allottee #2336; trust patent issued December 2, 1907 under Act of February 8, 1887

Francis Geisdorff allotted Lots 9,10,11,12 of Section 2 and N1/2 SW1/4, N1/2 SE1/4, N1/2 S1/2 SE1/4 of Section 11, T.1S, R.34E, 648.04 acres as homestead under Act of June 4, 1920; patent number 227584 signed by President Calvin Coolidge
Petition for Sale of Land by the Original Allottee (Frederick Geisdorff Jr.) received at Crow Agency
Thomas R. Powers made an offer to lease Frederick Geisdorff Jr.'s allotment and adjacent land owned by relatives
Appraisal made pertaining to Powers' request to lease the land; government farmer appraised land at $1194
Crow Superintendent C.H. Asbury wrote to Frederick Geisdorff Sr. sending leasing papers and requesting answer
Crow Agency offered for sale the allotment of Frederick Geisdorff Jr. on petition acknowledged before Supt. Kneale of Uintah and Ouray Agency
Acceptance of sale of allotment sent to Frederick Geisdorff Jr. for his signature
Frederick Geisdorff Jr. signed and returned the acceptance form to Crow office
Thomas R. Powers notified that owners of land offered under Crow Land Sale No. 991 had signed written acceptance of his bid of $1500 for 159.67 acres; balance of purchase price demanded
Checks for balance of sale price acknowledged as received by Superintendent; two checks from Powers for $1350 and $150 (advertising fee) on Sheridan Banking Company dated November 2, 1918
Superintendent wrote to Frederick Geisdorff Jr. noting he was only 20 years of age and doubting Indian Bureau would approve sale; requested birth date and names of relatives
Frederick Geisdorff Jr. died of influenza aged about 20, married, intestate, survived by wife Emily J. Geisdorff and minor daughter Emily Lucile Geisdorff
Superintendent Kneale wrote to Crow Agency stating Fritz died a few days ago of influenza
Superintendent Asbury wrote to Thomas R. Powers at Hartman Wyoming informing him of age issue and offering to cancel sale and return payment
Emily Lucile Geisdorff born, daughter of Frederick Geisdorff Jr. and Emily J. Geisdorff
Uintah and Ouray Agency notified Superintendent Asbury of birth of Emily Lucile Geisdorff, born May 23, 1918
Mrs. Emily J. Geisdorff wrote Superintendent at Crow Agency inquiring if Crow Agency could aid her in paying a note owed by her husband before his death
Superintendent answered Emily J. Geisdorff's letter regretting no funds available; noted sale pending but uncertain due to allottee's age; promised heirship hearing
Superintendent Asbury wrote to Commissioner of Indian Affairs reviewing all matters concerning the land sale, noting allottee born May 6, 1898, holding two checks from Powers for $1350 and $150
Superintendent Asbury wrote to Emily M. Geisdorff enclosing copy of letter to Commissioner and asking if she wished to have pending sale completed

Office of Indian Affairs responded to Asbury's March 24 letter stating nothing shows brother/sister want to sell allotments or widow wants sale to proceed; if heirs want sale to proceed, prepare and forward sale papers
Superintendent Asbury wrote to Mrs. Emily F. Geisdorff asking whether she wished sale completed to Powers for $1500 or bid rejected; enclosed authority to put old sale through
Superintendent Asbury enclosed sale papers to Commissioner stating he had just received acceptance of widow of allottee
General Land Office stated no reservations or withdrawals covering the land and no reasons why patent in fee should not be issued to purchaser; signed by John McPhaul, Acting Assistant Commissioner
Fee patent number 719146 issued by President Woodrow Wilson to Thomas R. Powers for 159.67 acres of Frederick Geisdorff Jr.'s allotment
Office of Indian Affairs transmitted fee patent 719146 to Superintendent Crow Agency for delivery to Thomas R. Powers upon signing receipt
Receipt for patent issued to Thomas R. Powers, purchaser from Frederick Geisdorff Jr., allotment No. 2336, 159.67 acres, patent No. 719146
Superintendent Asbury wrote to Thomas R. Powers enclosing copy of letter to Indian Bureau regarding Geisdorff sale
Superintendent Asbury wrote to Commissioner requesting completion of Land Sale No. 991, noting widow's acceptance and Powers' request for return of deposit if no action taken

Hearing held to determine heirs of Frederick Geisdorff Jr.; testimony taken by Superintendent Kneale at Fort Duchesne; determined heirs were wife Emily J. Geisdorff and daughter Emily Lucile Geisdorff
Report on heirship and recommendations transmitted to Secretary of Interior for estate of Frederick Geisdorff Jr., deceased Crow allottee No. 2336

Check 10613 for $1317.26 sent to Emily J. Geisdorff representing her share (one half plus dower interest) of estate proceeds; $188.90 credited to daughter Emily; $15.00 deducted for heirship fee from total of $1521.24
Crow Agency wrote to Mrs. Emily J. Geisdorff informing her that after heirs determined, proceeds of sale could be transferred to her agency; minor's shares cannot be paid without special authority

financial_transactions.payee: Estate of Frederick Geisdorff Jr. / Crow Agency
financial_transactions.description: Purchase of 159.67 acres of Crow allotment No. 2336 under Crow Land Sale No. 991; paid via two checks on Sheridan Banking Company: $1350.00 and $150.00 (advertising fee), dated November 2, 1918
Advertising fee for land sale No. 991 (purchase price over $1000 and under $2000)
Balance of purchase price for Frederick Geisdorff Jr. allotment, check on Sheridan Banking Company
Ten percent deposit submitted with bid for Crow Land Sale No. 991
Check 10613 representing Emily J. Geisdorff's share (one half plus dower interest) of estate of Frederick Geisdorff Jr.; total on books was $1521.24 less $15.00 heirship fee
Share of estate proceeds credited to minor daughter Emily Lucile Geisdorff
Heirship fee deducted from estate total of $1521.24, authorized by Indian Office
Bank account at Merchants National Bank of Billings derived from lease money deposited to credit of estate
Appraised value of Frederick Geisdorff Jr.'s 159.67 acre allotment as appraised by Government Farmer
Total amount on books at Crow Agency to credit of Frederick Geisdorff estate before deductions

correspondence.subject: Leasing papers for allotment
correspondence.action_requested: Answer regarding leasing of allotment

correspondence.subject: Doubts about approval of sale due to allottee's age
correspondence.action_requested: Submit date of birth and names and addresses of all relatives; submit statement from Superintendent Kneale recommending approval
correspondence.outcome: No reply received from allottee before his death

correspondence.subject: Acceptance of bid for Crow Land Sale No. 991
correspondence.action_requested: Remit balance due on purchase price within 30 days plus advertising fee; sign and return affidavit of vendee in duplicate
correspondence.outcome: Powers paid balance via two checks on November 2, 1918

correspondence.subject: Receipt of payment for Crow Land Sale No. 991
correspondence.action_requested: Report made to Indian Bureau recommending approval of sale
correspondence.outcome: Acknowledged receipt of checks for $1350 and $2.00

correspondence.subject: Death of Frederick Geisdorff Jr.
correspondence.action_requested: None - informational
correspondence.outcome: Crow Agency informed of allottee's death

correspondence.subject: Age issue with Frederick Geisdorff Jr. and proposed cancellation of sale
correspondence.action_requested: Powers may request return of amount paid; Frederick Geisdorff Jr. copied to submit information if he wishes
correspondence.outcome: Offered to cancel sale and return payment

correspondence.subject: Inquiry about assistance paying note owed by deceased husband
correspondence.action_requested: Financial assistance

correspondence.subject: Response to request for financial assistance; status of pending land sale
correspondence.action_requested: None immediate; heirship hearing to be advertised
correspondence.outcome: No funds available; sale uncertain due to age issue; heirship hearing to be held

correspondence.subject: Review of land sale matters for Frederick Geisdorff Jr. allotment No. 2336
correspondence.action_requested: Instructions on how to proceed with sale; holding checks from Powers for $1350 and $150

correspondence.subject: Pending sale of allotment; inquiry whether widow wishes sale completed
correspondence.action_requested: Let Superintendent know whether widow wishes pending sale completed

correspondence.sender: Chief Clerk, Office of Indian Affairs
correspondence.subject: Response to March 24 letter regarding death of Frederick Geisdorff Jr. and land sale
correspondence.action_requested: If probable heirs want sale to proceed, prepare and forward sale papers; otherwise reject bid and readvertise after heirs determined
correspondence.outcome: Sale papers prepared and forwarded after widow's acceptance received

correspondence.subject: Proposed sale of allotment of deceased husband Frederick Geisdorff Jr.
correspondence.action_requested: Sign enclosed authority if she wishes sale to proceed to Powers for $1500; write if she does not wish sale to go through

correspondence.subject: Enclosure of sale papers for allotment 2336; receipt of widow's acceptance
correspondence.action_requested: Approval of sale

correspondence.subject: Completion of Land Sale No. 991 - Frederick Geisdorff Jr. allotment
correspondence.action_requested: Approval of sale at early date; Powers requesting return of deposit if no action taken

correspondence.subject: Geisdorff sale - enclosing copy of letter to Indian Bureau
correspondence.action_requested: Informational

correspondence.sender_title: Acting Assistant Commissioner, General Land Office
correspondence.subject: Clearance for fee patent issuance for land in allotment No. 2336
correspondence.action_requested: None - clearance statement
correspondence.outcome: No reservations or withdrawals; no reasons why patent in fee should not be issued

correspondence.subject: Transmittal of fee patent No. 719146 to Thomas R. Powers
correspondence.action_requested: Make proper notations on records; deliver patent to Powers upon signing receipt in duplicate; forward original receipt to Washington

correspondence.subject: Fee patent No. 719146 issued November 14, 1919 to Thomas R. Powers on allotment No. 2336 of Frederick Geisdorff Jr.
correspondence.action_requested: Informational notification

correspondence.subject: Status of proceeds from sale of allotment; heirship determination pending
correspondence.action_requested: After heirs determined, widow may request transfer of proceeds to her agency; minor's shares require special authority

correspondence.subject: Birth of Emily Lucile Geisdorff
correspondence.action_requested: Informational
correspondence.outcome: Crow Agency informed of birth of heir

correspondence.subject: Heirship to estate of Frederick Geisdorff Jr., deceased Crow allottee No. 2336
correspondence.action_requested: Consideration of heirship report and recommendations

correspondence.subject: Distribution of estate proceeds of Frederick Geisdorff Jr.
correspondence.action_requested: Informational - enclosing check 10613 for $1317.26
correspondence.outcome: Emily J. Geisdorff received $1317.26; Emily Lucile Geisdorff credited $188.90

correspondence.subject: Inquiry about proceeds of sale and proposed investment in lands
correspondence.action_requested: Transfer of proceeds; investment in lands
correspondence.outcome: Informed proceeds cannot be paid until heirs determined; minor's shares require special authority

legislative_actions.outcome: Dawes Act - basis for trust patent issued December 2, 1907 for Frederick Geisdorff Jr.'s allotment
Act under which Frederick Geisdorff Jr.'s allotment was made, as amended
Act under which Francis Geisdorff's homestead allotment of 648.04 acres was made; trust period of 25 years established
Referenced in fee patent conditions for Thomas R. Powers regarding water rights and reclamation
Cited as basis for Crow Tribe's ownership claim to allotted lands upon allottee's death
